name: Cornbread-Stuffed Meatloaf
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large skillet, heat oil over medium heat. Add onion and sauté until tender.
2. Remove from heat; stir in cornbread crumbs and zucchini until well combined. Set aside to cool.
3. In a large bowl, combine ground beef, bread crumbs, egg whites, salt, basil, and red pepper. Shape into a 10-inch square on greased aluminum foil.
4. Spread half cup of parsley leaves over the center third of the meat square.
5. Gather and lightly press cornbread mixture into a 10-inch log. Place it over the parsley-covered center of the meat square.
6. Using foil, bring ends together to seal the meat and enclose the cornbread mixture. Press firmly and place seam side down on the foil in a shallow baking pan.
7. Cover top with foil. Bake at 350°F for 1 hour.
8. Uncover loaf and spread tomato sauce over top and sides. Bake an additional 15 minutes longer. This is also good cold.
